title: Missing privkey/pubkey/sig NULL checks in schnorr48_sign_frame
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/link/schnorr48.c
Lines: 259-298

schnorr48_sign_frame() validates dst_addr and payload but not privkey, pubkey, or sig parameters:

```c
int schnorr48_sign_frame(uint8_t epoch, uint16_t seqnum,
                         const uint8_t *dst_addr, size_t dst_addr_len,
                         const uint8_t *payload, size_t payload_len,
                         const uint8_t *privkey,   // Not validated
                         const uint8_t *pubkey,    // Not validated
                         uint8_t *sig)             // Not validated
{
    // Validates dst_addr_len and dst_addr/payload NULL checks
    // But privkey, pubkey, sig are used without checks
```

While these parameters are marked _Nonnull in the header, no runtime validation exists. A NULL privkey would cause crypto_sha512_update to hash garbage, and a NULL sig would cause memcpy to crash.

Impact: Crash if caller violates _Nonnull contracts. Less critical than verify since signing is internal code path, but defensive checks are good practice for C code.

Fix: Add NULL checks at function entry for privkey, pubkey, and sig parameters.
notes: Fixed in commit 805599c (project-LICHEN-vbex: added NULL checks for privkey, pubkey, sig in schnorr48_sign_frame per review). Working tree clean for this change.
close_reason: Conflicts resolved by preserving NULL checks in schnorr48_sign_frame and best changes from both branches in coap_dtn.c kiss_transport.c and spec; tests passed; merge completed
